preamble: The transformation of the high front rounded vowel [y] into the open central vowel [a] is a form of vowel lowering and unrounding, often documented in historical chain shifts or as a result of phonetic simplification in early language acquisition.

examples.note: Historical shift in northern Utah where rounded vowels moved downward to merge with the open central vowel [a].
Common phonetic simplification where complex front rounded vowels are replaced by the more easily articulated open central vowel.

sources: Phonetic research on early vowel acquisition and Utah dialectal mergers.
